expectations.text: Confirms the display_name then-clauses (200, returns updated display_name, persisted) are satisfied
expectations.evidence: It explicitly marks authenticated member, PATCH, 200 status, returned display_name, and persistence as satisfied.

expectations.text: Explicitly flags that the implementation drifted outside this scenario by adding things the scenario never called for: marketing_emails_opt_in handling (and sending an email) and theme_preference
expectations.evidence: It flags marketing_emails_opt_in, theme_preference, persistence, and marketing email side effect as drift.

expectations.text: Identifies the out-of-scope additions concretely by name rather than vaguely noting 'extra code'
expectations.evidence: The out-of-scope additions are named concretely.

expectations.text: Stays a focused gate scoped to this one scenario: does not turn into a full-feature audit or a principle-by-principle architecture critique
expectations.evidence: The output remains scoped to this scenario and does not become an architecture critique.
